scenario: Team needs auto-generated BPMN documentation for compliance. Wonders tooling.
question: BPMN documentation generation?

options.text: Automatic BPMN documentation generation is not possible -- this does not accurately reflect the behavior. BPMN XML files contain structured <bpmn:documentation> elements on process definitions and individual activities that can be extracted programmatically using any XML parsing library. Custom scripts can parse these elements and generate Markdown, HTML, or PDF documentation. io projects provide pre-built tooling. Documentation can also incorporate Element Template descriptions. BPMN diagram images can be rendered and included. For compliance, the generation can be integrated into CI pipelines triggered by Git commits or Web Modeler version changes.

Multiple approaches: documenting key process milestones in Camunda 8 can be achieved through several BPMN patterns. Intermediate Throw Events with Message or Signal semantics mark significant process checkpoints visible in Operate. BPMN Text Annotations provide contextual documentation without execution semantics. The documentation element on BPMN activities adds rich text notes visible in the modeler. Process variable updates at milestone points enable programmatic milestone tracking. The best approach depends on whether the milestone needs execution visibility, external notification, or purely documentary purposes.

Manual documentation writing is the baseline, but automation significantly improves consistency and timeliness. BPMN XML files contain structured <bpmn:documentation> elements on process definitions and individual activities that can be extracted programmatically. Custom scripts using standard XML parsing libraries can traverse BPMN files, extract documentation content, and generate Markdown, HTML, or PDF outputs. Community tools provide pre-built BPMN-to-documentation generation. CI pipelines can auto-generate and publish documentation on every process change.

While the Camunda Modeler (both Web and Desktop versions) allows adding documentation text to process elements in the properties panel, it is not the only tool for documentation generation. The structured <bpmn:documentation> XML elements written in the Modeler can be extracted by any script or tool that parses XML. js scripts can generate richer documentation formats. The Modeler provides the authoring experience but documentation generation can be done externally from the BPMN files it produces.

a.text: Incorrect. BPMN documentation generation: Sources of
